Name: test_toolkit_credential_indicators_e2e
Description:
E2E test: verify all indicators and fix invalid credentials on Toolkit.

        Flow:
        1. Create Jira credential with invalid token
        2. Create toolkit with that credential
        3. Verify all indicators:
           - Status indicator with "Authentication failed:" tooltip
           - Warning message with "Authentication failed:" text
           - Reload button with "Reload and apply changes" tooltip
           - Open in new tab button with "Open in new tab" tooltip
           - Save button is disabled
        4. Click "Open in new tab" and verify new tab opens
        5. Fix credential via API
        6. Close the new tab and return to original
        7. Click Reload
        8. Verify after fix:
           - Status indicator gone
           - Warning message gone
           - Reload button gone
           - Open in new tab remains
           - Save button enabled

Name: test_context_budget_reflects_profile_max_tokens[32k_tokens]
Description:
Context Budget panel in chat shows the max tokens set in profile.

        - P2 (32,000 tokens): Updated value propagates (not cached)

        Steps:
            1. Navigate to profile settings.
            2. Enable context management for new conversations.
            3. Set Max Context Tokens to the parameterized value.
            4. Navigate to chat and create a new conversation.
            5. Send a short message to trigger Context Budget panel.
            6. Verify the panel is visible.
            7. Verify the max tokens displayed equals the configured value.

        Cleanup: deletes the created conversation via API.

Verify that creating and deleting conversations via the API works.

        This is a smoke test for the fixture's create/delete cycle.

Description: Create a credential through the UI form for any toolkit type.

Name: test_export_pipeline_if_available
Description:
PIPE-029: Export pipeline via menu (skips if not available).

        Attempts to export the pipeline.  If the Export menu item is not
        present, the test is skipped (feature not yet implemented).

Name: test_delete_pipeline_via_ui_menu
Description: Create a pipeline, delete via the UI three-dot menu, and verify removal.

Name: test_agent_search
Description: Search for an agent by name on the dashboard.

Name: test_create_agent_via_ui
Description:
Create an agent through the UI form and verify it appears in the list.

        Steps:
        1. Navigate to create agent page
        2. Fill in name + description + instructions
        3. Click Save
        4. Verify user lands on the agent detail page
        5. Navigate back to agents list
        6. Verify agent appears in the list

Name: test_chat_with_toolkit[confluence]
Description: Add toolkit to chat, send a message, verify tool execution.

Name: test_pipeline_dashboard_loads
Description: PIPE-001: Dashboard loads with header and search input.

Name: test_agent_with_toolkit_executes_in_chat
Description:
Create agent with toolkit, add agent as participant in chat, verify toolkit executes.

        The toolkit is attached to the agent (not the conversation directly).
        This tests the agent-owns-toolkit execution path in real chat (/app/chat).

        Steps:
        1. Navigate to agent detail page
        2. Add GitHub toolkit to agent via UI
        3. Verify toolkit is attached, then save and wait for persistence
        4. Navigate to conversation and let page settle
        5. Add agent as participant
        6. Send message asking to list branches
        7. Wait for AI + toolkit response to stabilise
        8. Verify response contains branch/repository data from toolkit

Name: test_pipeline_credential_indicators_e2e
Description:
E2E test: verify all indicators and fix invalid credentials on Pipeline.

        Flow:
        1. Create Jira credential with invalid token
        2. Create toolkit with that credential
        3. Create pipeline and add toolkit node
        4. Verify all indicators:
           - Status indicator with "Authentication failed:" tooltip
           - Warning message with "Authentication failed:" text
           - Reload button with "Reload and apply changes" tooltip
           - Open in new tab button with "Open in new tab" tooltip
        5. Click "Open in new tab" and verify new tab opens
        6. Fix credential via API
        7. Close the new tab and return to original
        8. Click Reload
        9. Verify after fix:
           - Status indicator gone
           - Warning message gone
           - Reload button gone
           - Open in new tab remains

Name: test_new_chat_creates_fresh_session
Description:
New Chat button starts a fresh support session.

        Steps:
        1. Open Support Assistant
        2. Send a message to create conversation
        3. Click New Chat
        4. Verify old conversation moved to history
        5. Verify clean slate (welcome or empty state)

        Covers: 3.5.1, 3.5.2

Name: test_create_credential[gitlab]
Description: Create a credential through the UI form for any toolkit type.

Name: test_yaml_content_reflects_pipeline
Description:
PIPE-020: YAML content should reflect the pipeline structure.

        A pipeline with an LLM node should have YAML content containing
        node definitions and entry_point.

Name: test_internal_tools_enum_usage
Description:
Verify internal tools can be toggled using the enum-based API.

        Note: This is a smoke test for the toggle_tool() API accepting enum values.
        Full state verification (checking if checkbox actually changed) is not
        performed due to MUI DOM complexity making is_tool_enabled() unreliable.
        See test_internal_tools_enable_disable (skipped) for details.

        This test verifies:
        - toggle_tool() accepts InternalTool enum values
        - Method completes without errors
        - Can be called multiple times without breaking

Name: test_hash_search_participants
Description:
TC-CHAT-017: Use # to search participants.

        Verifies typing # triggers participant search dropdown.
        The hash search feature detects '#' via keydown events, so we must
        use type() or press_sequentially() instead of fill() to trigger it.

Name: test_empty_pipeline_execution
Description:
PIPE-015: Executing a pipeline with no nodes (only END).

        An empty pipeline should either return an error message or produce
        some response — it should not hang or crash the UI.

Name: test_delete_conversation_with_confirmation
Description:
TC-CONV-007: Delete conversation via UI and verify removal.

        Verifies:
        - Three-dot menu → Delete flow (TC-CONV-007 UI)
        - Confirmation dialog appears with title and buttons
        - Conversation removed from sidebar after confirmation
        - Conversation removed from API (TC-CONV-007 API coverage)

        Flow:
            1. Create a conversation via the API
            2. Navigate to the chat page, verify it appears in the sidebar
            3. Hover the conversation → open three-dot menu → click Delete
            4. Verify confirmation dialog appears with title and Cancel/Delete buttons
            5. Click Delete to confirm
            6. Verify conversation is removed from the sidebar
            7. Verify the API confirms the conversation is deleted

Name: test_cannot_send_empty_message
Description:
TC-CHAT-007: Cannot send empty message.

        Verifies pressing Enter with empty input does not send a message.
        Note: The current UI doesn't show a send button until there's text,
        so we test by pressing Enter on empty input.

Name: test_create_github_credential
Description:
Create a GitHub credential through the UI form.

        1. Navigate to /app/credentials/create-credential/github
        2. Fill Display Name, select Token auth, enter access token
        3. Click Save
        4. Verify URL redirects to /app/credentials/all
        5. Verify credential card appears with correct name

Name: test_navigate_away_and_reexecute
Description:
PIPE-016: Navigate away from the pipeline and return to re-execute.

Name: test_welcome_message_counter_visible_and_updates_realtime
Description:
Character counter appears when typing and updates in real-time.
        TC-1688, TC-1685
        Acceptance Criteria #1: Real-time character counter visible.

        Steps:
        1. Type initial text - counter appears
        2. Add more text - counter updates (remaining chars decreases)

Name: test_voice_settings_not_visible_by_default
Description:
TC5: Voice settings are NOT displayed in Chat by default.

        Regression test for bug #5235: Read-out control was inappropriately
        visible in default Chat window state.

        Steps:
            1. Navigate to Chat, create new conversation
            2. Send "hi" message and wait for AI response
            3. Verify Voice Mini Player is NOT visible after response by default

        Expected:
            Voice settings controls should only appear when Read-out and
            Voice mode features are explicitly activated.

        Related:
            - Bug: https://github.com/EliteaAI/elitea_issues/issues/5235

Name: test_navigate_between_conversations
Description:
Navigate from one conversation to another via the sidebar list.

        Creates two conversations, opens the chat page, clicks the first
        to open it, then clicks the second to switch. Takes screenshots
        to prove each conversation was loaded.

        Uses ID-based selection for reliability.
